title: Omnigent: The Control Plane for Enterprise Agent Ecosystems
summary: Omnigent is an open-source meta-harness that sits above existing agent frameworks and coding agents, enabling organizations to combine, control, and share heterogeneous agents through a unified abstraction layer. It introduces policy-driven governance, budget controls, permission management, sandboxed execution, multi-agent composition, and real-time collaborative workflows. By decoupling orchestration and control from individual agent harnesses, Omnigent enables interoperable, enterprise-scale agent ecosystems while reducing framework lock-in and operational complexity.
category: Agent Infrastructure + Agent Orchestration + Multi-Agent Systems + AI Governance

title: Continual Learning Bench (CL-Bench): Evaluating Frontier AI Systems in Stateful Environments
summary: CL-Bench introduces a comprehensive benchmark for measuring whether frontier AI systems can learn from experience, accumulate knowledge, transfer insights across tasks, and continuously improve within persistent stateful environments. The benchmark exposes a critical gap between model capability and adaptive intelligence, revealing that even advanced AI systems struggle with long-horizon learning, knowledge consolidation, and sustained performance improvement over time.
category: Continual Learning + Adaptive AI + Agent Evaluation

title: Anthropic — Introducing Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5
summary: Anthropic's latest frontier models represent a significant step toward enterprise-scale cognitive systems, advancing reasoning depth, reliability, long-context understanding, and agentic execution. The release reflects the broader industry transition from standalone language models to production-grade AI systems capable of powering complex workflows, decision intelligence, and next-generation enterprise applications.
category: Frontier Models + Agentic AI + Enterprise AI Systems

title: LEAP: Advancing Formal Mathematical Intelligence Through Agentic AI Systems
summary: LEAP introduces an agentic framework that enhances LLM performance on formal mathematics through theorem proving, iterative reasoning, tool use, and self-improvement loops, highlighting the next frontier of AI capabilities in complex symbolic reasoning.

title: The Rise of Personal AI Infrastructure: Scaling Millions of Persistent Models on Trillion-Parameter Foundations
summary: This research explores how parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) can evolve into a scalable infrastructure layer for maintaining millions of personalized AI models on top of shared trillion-parameter foundation models, enabling persistent and individualized intelligence at scale.

title: Mid-Training: The Emerging Strategic Layer of Foundation Model Development
summary: Mid-training is becoming a critical stage between pre-training and post-training, enabling foundation models to acquire domain expertise, reasoning capabilities, and specialized knowledge more efficiently than scaling pre-training alone.

title: Beyond Static Benchmarks: Evaluating Continual Intelligence in Real-World Stateful AI Systems
summary: Continual Learning Bench introduces a new framework for evaluating how AI systems adapt, retain knowledge, and learn over time, moving beyond static benchmarks toward measuring continual intelligence in dynamic environments.

title: The Data Agent Frontier: Measuring AI's Ability to Reason, Retrieve, and Deliver Business Intelligence
summary: DataAgentBench evaluates AI agents across realistic business intelligence workflows, measuring their ability to retrieve information, perform analytical reasoning, and generate actionable enterprise insights.

title: Vector Lakebase: Zilliz’s AI-Native Data Foundation for Agentic Systems
summary: Zilliz introduced Vector Lakebase, a next-generation AI data architecture that unifies data lake scalability with vector database performance, supporting enterprise RAG, multimodal AI, and continuously evolving agentic systems.
category: AI Data Infrastructure + Agentic AI Platforms
